title: Lithocarpus clathratus
taxonomy: Lithocarpus clathratus is classified as a species of tree in the family Fagaceae, commonly known as the beech family. It belongs to the genus Lithocarpus, which is a group of deciduous and evergreen trees native to Southeast Asia. The family Fagaceae is further classified under the order Fagales, and the class Magnoliopsida, which includes a wide range of flowering plants. The class Magnoliopsida is part of the kingdom Plantae, which comprises all types of plants. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Lithocarpus clathratus with other plants.
uses: Lithocarpus clathratus, also known as the Taiwan red oak, has been found to possess medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used in East Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The extracts from Lithocarpus clathratus have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Studies have also suggested that the plant may have potential in the treatment of certain types of cancer. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Lithocarpus clathratus.
benefits: Lithocarpus clathratus, also known as the Taiwan copper beech or Taiwan evergreen holly, has been traditionally used in Asian medicine for its potential health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. The plant is also thought to have antimicrobial properties, which could aid in the treatment of infections and promote wound healing. Additionally, Lithocarpus clathratus may have a potential role in lowering cholesterol levels and reducing blood pressure. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits and potential uses of Lithocarpus clathratus.

parts: Lithocarpus clathratus is a species of plant that is native to Southeast Asia, and its medicinal parts are primarily its leaves and bark. preparations: Lithocarpus clathratus is a plant species native to China, and it is used in traditional medicine.
